verses.text: 'As the hart panteth after the water brooks, so panteth my soul after thee, O God.' He thirsts for the living God. The deepest longing of the believer, even in distress, is not for relief but for God Himself.

'As the hart panteth after the water brooks, so panteth my soul after thee, O God.' He thirsts for the living God. The deepest longing of the believer, even in distress, is not for relief but for God Himself.

His tears are his food day and night while men ask, 'Where is thy God?'; he remembers leading the throng to worship. Memory of past joy sharpens present grief. Recalling sweeter days with God can both wound and revive the soul.

His tears are his food day and night while men ask, 'Where is thy God?'; he remembers leading the throng to worship. Memory of past joy sharpens present grief. Recalling sweeter days with God can both wound and revive the soul.
He reasons with himself: 'Why art thou cast down, O my soul?... hope thou in God.' He preaches to his own heart. When feeling sinks, faith speaks back, calling the soul to hope and praise.
He reasons with himself: 'Why art thou cast down, O my soul?... hope thou in God.' He preaches to his own heart. When feeling sinks, faith speaks back, calling the soul to hope and praise.

Trouble rolls over him like waves, 'deep calleth unto deep,' yet 'the LORD will command his lovingkindness in the daytime.' Sorrow and song mingle. Even in floods of grief, God appoints His mercy by day and His song by night.

Trouble rolls over him like waves, 'deep calleth unto deep,' yet 'the LORD will command his lovingkindness in the daytime.' Sorrow and song mingle. Even in floods of grief, God appoints His mercy by day and His song by night.

He asks his Rock why He has forgotten him, mourning under his foes, then again charges his soul to hope in God. The wrestling repeats. Faith may question and grieve, yet keeps returning to hope in the God of its salvation.

He asks his Rock why He has forgotten him, mourning under his foes, then again charges his soul to hope in God. The wrestling repeats. Faith may question and grieve, yet keeps returning to hope in the God of its salvation.
